source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure That Cloud SQL Database Instances Are Configured With Automated Backups

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure That Cloud SQL Database Instances Are Configured With Automated Backups
input_row.description: It is recommended to have all SQL database instances set to enable automated backups.
input_row.rationale: Backups provide a way to restore a Cloud SQL instance to recover lost data or recover from a problem with that instance. Automated backups need to be set for any instance that contains data that should be protected from loss or damage. This recommendation is applicable for SQL Server, PostgreSql, MySql generation 1 and MySql generation 2 instances. Impact: Automated Backups will increase required size of storage and costs associated with it.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the Cloud SQL Instances page in the Google Cloud Console by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances. 2. Click the instance name to open its instance details page. 3. Go to the Backups menu. 4. Ensure that Automated backups is set to Enabled and Backup time is mentioned. From Google Cloud CLI 1. List all Cloud SQL database instances using the following command: gcloud sql instances list --format=json | jq '. | map(select(.instanceType != "READ_REPLICA_INSTANCE")) | .[].name' NOTE: gcloud command has been added with the filter to exclude read-replicas instances, as GCP do not provide Automated Backups for read-replica instances.   2. Ensure that the below command returns True for every Cloud SQL database instance. gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME> -- format="value('Enabled':settings.backupConfiguration.enabled)"
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to the Cloud SQL Instances page in the Google Cloud Console by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/sql/instances. 2. Select the instance where the backups need to be configured. 3. Click Edit. 4. In the Backups section, check `Enable automated backups', and choose a backup window. 5. Click Save. From Google Cloud CLI 1. List all Cloud SQL database instances using the following command: gcloud sql instances list --format=json | jq '. | map(select(.instanceType != "READ_REPLICA_INSTANCE")) | .[].name' NOTE: gcloud command has been added with the filter to exclude read-replicas instances, as GCP do not provide Automated Backups for read-replica instances. 2. Enable Automated backups for every Cloud SQL database instance using the below command: gcloud sql instances patch <INSTANCE_NAME> --backup-start-time <[HH:MM]> The backup-start-time parameter is specified in 24-hour time, in the UTC±00 time zone, and specifies the start of a 4-hour backup window. Backups can start any time during the backup window. Default Value: By default, automated backups are not configured for Cloud SQL instances. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/mysql/backup-recovery/backups 2. https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/postgres/backup-recovery/backups 3. https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/sqlserver/backup-recovery/backups 4. https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/mysql/backup-recovery/backing-up 5. https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/postgres/backup-recovery/backing-up 6. https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/sqlserver/backup-recovery/backing-up
input_row.impact: Automated Backups will increase required size of storage and costs associated with it.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
This control can be fully verified via GCP APIs/Google Cloud SDK (gcloud)/GCP CLI as detailed in the audit and remediation sections. The existence and configuration of automated backups for Cloud SQL instances are properties that can be programmatically checked using GCP CLI commands or Google Cloud SDK (gcloud). 

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_sql_instance_automated_backups_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **GCP CLI Commands:** Use `gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME> --format="value(settings.backupConfiguration.enabled)"` to verify that automated backups are enabled.
- **Resources to Enumerate:** List all Cloud SQL instances using `gcloud sql instances list --format=json | jq '. | map(select(.instanceType != "READ_REPLICA_INSTANCE")) | .[].name'`.
- **Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:** Check the `settings.backupConfiguration.enabled` property for each SQL instance to ensure it is set to True.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:** 
  - **Pass:** If the `settings.backupConfiguration.enabled` is true for each instance.
  - **Fail:** If the `settings.backupConfiguration.enabled` is false or not set for any instance.
- **Example:** Use `gcloud sql instances describe <INSTANCE_NAME>` and confirm that the JSON output includes `"backupConfiguration": {"enabled": true}` for all instances that are not read replicas.

**SOURCE:** CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
